text:
Explore (Digging In)

Providing for Individual Needs

Have Learning Aid Template: Blank 100s Grids and Learning Aid Template: Blank Grids and tape Diagrams available for students to use as needed.

Suggested Questions

In the Initial Challenge, you can assess how students are making progress at connecting ratio reasoning to percent reasoning.

  • What would a grid or tape diagram for each assignment look like?
Answers will vary. See some student examples that follow.
• Are there other strategies that you could use?
Yes, write comparison statements and scale them to 100 in the whole, make a ratio table, or make a double number line.

Planning for the Summary

What evidence will you use in the summary to clarify and deepen understanding of the Now What Do You Know? question?What will you do if you do not have evidence?

Now What Do You Know?

How is percent used when making comparison statements? How are these the same as or different from other comparison statements?

As students are working, look for examples where students see the power of percent ratios when comparing quantities relative to the total and how using percents lets you compare ratios by finding equivalent ratios for quantities out of 100.
